title: castilleja flava var. rustica
intro: Castilleja flava var. rustica, also known as Yellow Indian Paintbrush, is a perennial plant species in the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly as an expectorant and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant contains bioactive compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its health benefits, including antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory properties. Castilleja flava var. rustica can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and its leaves and flowers can be used in herbal remedies for respiratory and digestive issues, among others.
